title: Walkeshwar Temple
tagline: Walkeshwar Temple — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: Walkeshwar Temple, also known as the (Baan Ganga Temple), is an Ancient Hindu temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva located in Walkeshwar, near Malabar Hill neighbourhood, in South Mumba
overview.description: Walkeshwar Temple, also known as the (Baan Ganga Temple), is an Ancient Hindu temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva located in Walkeshwar, near Malabar Hill neighbourhood, in South Mumbai precinct of the city of Mumbai, India. It is situated at the highest point of the city, and close to the temple lies the Banganga Tank.

nearestAirport.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Banganga Tank
topPlaces.description: The Banganga Tank is a temple tank which is part of the Hindu Walkeshwar Temple complex in the Malabar Hill area of the city of Mumbai, India.

topPlaces.name: Walkeshwar
topPlaces.description: Walkeshwar (Pronunciation: [ʋaːɭkeʃʋəɾ]) is an affluent area in South Mumbai, India, at the north-western end of the Marine Drive loop. It has a large Gujarati population.

topPlaces.name: Lok Bhavan, Mumbai
topPlaces.description: The Lok Bhavan is located in 20 hectares (49 acres) of sylvan surroundings, surrounded on three sides by the sea. The estate has several heritage bungalows, trees, large lawns and a beach.

topPlaces.name: Malabar Hill
topPlaces.description: Malabar Hill is amongst the most affluent residential areas in Mumbai. It is home to several business tycoons and film personalities.

topPlaces.name: F. D. Alpaiwalla Museum
topPlaces.description: The Framji Dadabhoy Alpaiwalla Museum is an archaeological and ethnographic museum in the city of Mumbai, India that contains artifacts relating to the history and heritage of India's Zoroastrian community. Set up and maintained by the Bombay Parsi Punchayet, the museum consists of two sections: the F. D. Alpaiwalla Collection and archaeological finds from Iran donated by Zoroastrian priest and archaeologist Ervad Jamshed Unvala.

topPlaces.name: Jinnah House
topPlaces.description: South Court Mansion, also known as the Jinnah House, is an unoccupied house in Malabar Hill, a premium neighbourhood of the city of Mumbai in India associated with Muhammad Ali Jinnah, the founder and first governor-general of Pakistan, and his family. It was built by Jinnah and remained his main residence for 10 years until he left in 1946 for Karachi.

topPlaces.name: All Saints' Church, Malabar Hill
topPlaces.description: All Saints' Church on Little Gibbs Road in Malabar Hill, Mumbai, India is affiliated to the Church of North India. It was built in 1881 as an Anglican church for the local congregation that had to earlier travel some 8 kilometres to St.

topPlaces.name: Kamala Nehru Park, Mumbai
topPlaces.description: Kamala Nehru Park is part of the Hanging Gardens complex in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India covering an area of approximate 16,000 sq.mt. (4 acres).

items.note: The Banganga Tank is a temple tank which is part of the Hindu Walkeshwar Temple complex in the Malabar Hill area of the city of Mumbai, India.
Walkeshwar (Pronunciation: [ʋaːɭkeʃʋəɾ]) is an affluent area in South Mumbai, India, at the north-western end of the Marine Drive loop. It has a large Gujarati population.
The Lok Bhavan is located in 20 hectares (49 acres) of sylvan surroundings, surrounded on three sides by the sea. The estate has several heritage bungalows, trees, large lawns and a beach.
Malabar Hill is amongst the most affluent residential areas in Mumbai. It is home to several business tycoons and film personalities.

The Framji Dadabhoy Alpaiwalla Museum is an archaeological and ethnographic museum in the city of Mumbai, India that contains artifacts relating to the history and heritage of India's Zoroastrian community. Set up and maintained by the Bombay Parsi Punchayet, the museum consists of two sections: the F. D. Alpaiwalla Collection and archaeological finds from Iran donated by Zoroastrian priest and archaeologist Ervad Jamshed Unvala.

South Court Mansion, also known as the Jinnah House, is an unoccupied house in Malabar Hill, a premium neighbourhood of the city of Mumbai in India associated with Muhammad Ali Jinnah, the founder and first governor-general of Pakistan, and his family. It was built by Jinnah and remained his main residence for 10 years until he left in 1946 for Karachi.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — All Saints' Church, Malabar Hill & around

items.note: All Saints' Church on Little Gibbs Road in Malabar Hill, Mumbai, India is affiliated to the Church of North India. It was built in 1881 as an Anglican church for the local congregation that had to earlier travel some 8 kilometres to St.
Kamala Nehru Park is part of the Hanging Gardens complex in Mumbai, Maharashtra, India covering an area of approximate 16,000 sq.mt. (4 acres).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Walkeshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Walkeshwar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Walkeshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (18 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Walkeshwar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Walkeshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Walkeshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Walkeshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Walkeshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Banganga Tank, Walkeshwar, Lok Bhavan, Mumbai, Malabar Hill, F. D. Alpaiwalla Museum.
